generatedSentence: Elara, an orphan in the modern, industrial city, woke up with familiar worries. The constant hum of machinery made her feel wearied. She often found herself idling by the great lakes that reflected the heliocentric horizon, stretching towards the hinterland. Her only solace was a complex diagram of a giant blossom, an impossible vegetable she'd found in an ancient text, viewed through a strange, lunate oculus. One day, news spread that a powerful entity sought to defile the sacred hill where the city's power source lay, a place long defamed by rumor. Elara felt a deep irritated sense of injustice. A wraith-like glare emanated from the ichor-stained machinery, giving her pause. She knew she couldn't misjudge the threat. The city's leaders, blind to the true danger, dismissed her caution. They seemed to be in a constant state of versus the truth, haunted by a ghosting presence that was spreading like gangrene through the bestiary of their ancient beliefs. Even the city's massive camels seemed uneasy. To avert catastrophe, Elara had to become a gladiator of sorts. She faced a monstrous tamer who commanded legions of lizards from a diorama-like castle high in the mountains. Her powerful biceps strained as she climbed the perilous cascade of rocks, her hands grasping for purchase. A tester from her past, now offered cryptic telemetry about the foe's weakness, a single tooth that could bring it down. The air was thick with vapor as she fought, fueled by her resolve and a handful of cranberries for energy, inching towards triumph. With the threat neutralized, Elara, though still macular with exhaustion, felt a witty sense of accomplishment. She had saved not just the granary but the very soul of her people. The city's mason began rebuilding, and a peculiar dugong was found swimming in the now-clean lacunar waters. A llama, once a creature of myth, now grazed peacefully. The city celebrated with an opulent opera, a grand performance filled with olive branches and new beginnings. Elara’s waking thoughts were no longer plagued by the quiet mystery of the world, leaving behind the vermin of doubt. She saw a future where basketball games could be played without fear, a true voucher for peace.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, cyberpunk graphic novel style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is Elara, wearing a worn, patched tech-suit with glowing blue circuitry accents, carrying a staff tipped with a pulsating green crystal, her short, spiky black hair framing intense green eyes. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elara, an orphan, looking out over a sprawling, polluted industrial city with glowing lakes, holding a holographic diagram, feeling weary, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elara confronting a mysterious, shadowy entity near a defiled hill with ichor-stained machinery, expressing caution, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elara as a gladiator, climbing a treacherous, cascading rock face towards a distant castle, straining her biceps, dynamic action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elara observing a peaceful, rebuilt city with a dugong in clean lacunar waters and a llama grazing, holding an olive branch, medium wide shot. All panels feature cel-shading, bold ink outlines, and cinematic lighting. NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue.

definitions.definition: To make unclean, dirty, or impure; soil; befoul.
To vandalize or add inappropriate contents to something considered sacred or special; desecrate
definitions.example: To urinate on someone's grave is an example of a way to defile it.

definitions.definition: To deprive or ruin someone's (sexual) purity or chastity, often not consensually; stain; tarnish; mar; rape
definitions.example: The serial rapist kidnapped and defiled a six-year-old girl.

definitions.definition: To disgrace; to bring into disrepute.
To charge; to accuse (someone) of an offence.
To harm or diminish the reputation of; to disparage.
definitions.example: to defame somebody

definitions.definition: A plant-eating aquatic marine mammal, of the genus Dugong, found in tropical regions.
A three-dimensional display of a scenery, often having a painted background in front of which models are arranged, e.g. in a museum where stuffed animals are presented against a painted landscape.
A plan, drawing, sketch or outline to show how something works, or show the relationships between the parts of a whole.
definitions.example: Electrical diagrams show device interconnections.

definitions.definition: A graph or chart.

A functor from an index category to another category. The objects and morphisms of the index category need not have any internal substance, but rather merely outline the connective structure of at least some part of the diagram's codomain. If the index category is J and the codomain is C, then the diagram is said to be "of type J in C".
To represent or indicate something using a diagram.
To schedule the operations of a locomotive or train according to a diagram.
To make or to become weary.
A strong feeling of anxiety.
definitions.example: I'm afflicted by worry throughout the night.

definitions.definition: An instance or cause of such a feeling.
definitions.example: My main worry is that I'll miss the train.

definitions.definition: To be troubled; to give way to mental anxiety or doubt.
definitions.example: Stop worrying about your test, it’ll be fine.

definitions.definition: Disturb the peace of mind of; afflict with mental agitation or distress.
definitions.example: Your tone of voice worries me.

definitions.definition: To harass; to irritate or distress.
definitions.example: The President was worried into military action by persistent advisors.

definitions.definition: To seize or shake by the throat, especially of a dog or wolf.
definitions.example: Your dog’s been worrying sheep again.

definitions.definition: To touch repeatedly, to fiddle with.
To strangle.
(often followed by up) To stop sleeping.
definitions.example: I woke up at four o'clock this morning.

definitions.definition: (often followed by up) To make somebody stop sleeping; to rouse from sleep.
definitions.example: The neighbour's car alarm woke me from a strange dream.

definitions.definition: To put in motion or action; to arouse; to excite.
To be excited or roused up; to be stirred up from a dormant, torpid, or inactive state; to be active.
To lay out a body prior to burial in order to allow family and friends to pay their last respects.
To watch, or sit up with, at night, as a dead body.
To be or remain awake; not to sleep.
To be alert; to keep watch
definitions.example: Command unto the guards that they diligently wake.

definitions.definition: To sit up late for festive purposes; to hold a night revel.
The act of becoming awake from sleep, or a period of time spent awake.
Occurring during wakefulness.
Wise, having good judgement.
Possessing a strong intellect or intellectual capacity; intelligent, skilful, ingenious.
Clever; amusingly ingenious.
definitions.example: His speech was both witty and informative.

definitions.definition: Full of wit.
definitions.example: His frequent quips mark him as particularly witty.

definitions.definition: Quick of mind; insightful; in possession of wits.
definitions.example: She may have grown older, but she has grown no less witty.

definitions.definition: A ghost or specter, especially a person's likeness seen just after their death.
To make an error in judging, to incorrectly assess.
definitions.example: I misjudged you. I don't like your politics but I appreciate your loyalty to your friends.

definitions.definition: Something secret or unexplainable; an unknown.
definitions.example: The truth behind the events remains a mystery.

definitions.definition: Someone or something with an obscure or puzzling nature.
definitions.example: That man is a mystery.

definitions.definition: A secret or mystical meaning.
A religious truth not understandable by the application of human reason alone (without divine aid).
(obsolete outside Eastern Orthodoxy) A sacrament.
(chiefly in plural) A secret religious celebration, admission to which was usually through initiation.
definitions.example: the Eleusinian mysteries

definitions.definition: A particular event or series of events in the life of Christ.
definitions.example: The second decade of the Rosary concerns the Sorrowful mysteries, such as the crucifixion and the crowning with thorns.

definitions.definition: A craft, art or trade; specifically a guild of craftsmen.
A bricklayer, one whose occupation is to build with stone or brick
One who prepares stone for building purposes.
A member of the fraternity of Freemasons. See Freemason.
(normally with a preposition) To build stonework or brickwork about, under, in, over, etc.; to construct by masons
definitions.example: to mason in a kettle or boiler

definitions.definition: Someone who lives in modern times.
Pertaining to a current or recent time and style; not ancient.
definitions.example: Our online interactive game is a modern approach to teaching about gum disease.  Although it was built in the 1600s, the building still has a very modern look.

definitions.definition: (history) Pertaining to the modern period (c.1800 to contemporary times), particularly in academic historiography.
Relating to the macula, the area of the retina responsible for detailed central vision.
To provoke impatience, anger, or displeasure in.
To cause or induce displeasure or irritation.
To induce pain in (all or part of a body or organism).
To render null and void.
Experiencing a feeling of irritation.
Inflamed and painful.
To spend in idleness; to waste; to consume.
To lose or spend time doing nothing, or without being employed in business.
Of an engine: to run at a slow speed, or out of gear; to tick over.
An idle period; something done idly.
(19th-mid 20th century) An employee in industry.
An enterprise producing tangible goods or providing certain services to industrial companies.
A bond or stock issued by such a company.
A film made for use within an industry, not for a movie-going audience.
Industrial music.
definitions.example: I wish they'd play more industrial in this club.

definitions.definition: An industrial piercing.
Of or relating to industry, notably manufacturing.
definitions.example: The industrial segment of the economy has seen troubles lately.

definitions.definition: Produced by such industry.
definitions.example: Handicraft is less standardized then industrial products, hence less artistic or rather flawless.

definitions.definition: Used by such industry.
Suitable for use in such industry; industrial-grade.
definitions.example: This is an industrial product—it's much too strong for home use.

definitions.definition: Massive in scale or quantity.
Employed as manpower by such industry.
(of a society or country) Having many industries; industrialized.
definitions.example: Italy is a part-industrial, part-rural nation.

definitions.definition: Belonging or pertaining to the genre of industrial music.
definitions.example: a track with clashing industrial beats

definitions.definition: (followed by a preposition) To advance very slowly, or by a small amount (in a particular direction).
definitions.example: Fearful of falling, he inched along the window ledge.

definitions.definition: To drive by inches, or small degrees.
To deal out by inches; to give sparingly.
Very gradual movement.
The liquid that in Greek mythology was said to flow in place of blood in the veins of the gods
Any bloodlike fluid
A watery, fetid discharge from a sore
Yellow bile
(in ancient Rome) A person (professional or slave) who entertained the public by engaging in mortal combat with another, or with a wild animal.
(by extension) A disputant in a public controversy or debate.
A professional boxer.
To fight as entertainment for others.
To compete in a public contest.
To debate or argue.
To act aggressively toward others.
A storage facility for grain or sometimes animal feed.
A fertile, grain-growing region.
The necrosis or rotting of flesh, usually caused by lack of blood supply.
definitions.example: If gangrene sets in, we may have to amputate the foot.

definitions.definition: A damaging or corrupting influence.
To produce gangrene in.
To be affected with gangrene.
To corrupt; To cause to become degenerate.
To haunt; to appear to in the form of an apparition.
To die; to expire.
To ghostwrite.
To sail seemingly without wind.
To copy a file or hard drive image.
To gray out (a visual item) to indicate that it is unavailable.
To forcibly disconnect an IRC user who is using one's reserved nickname.
To appear without warning; to move quickly and quietly; to slip.
To kill.
To break up with someone without warning or explanation; to perform an act of ghosting.
The practice of hiding prisoners from inspection from (possibly hostile) outside inspectors.
The blurry appearance of a television picture resulting from interference caused by multipath reception.
Ghost imaging.
A form of identity theft in which someone steals the identity, and sometimes even the role within society, of a specific dead person (the "ghost") who is not widely known to be deceased.
A problem with a keyboard where certain simultaneous keypresses trigger the action of a further key that was not in fact pressed.
A method of ending a personal relationship by stopping any contact with the other party and not providing an explanation.
definitions.example: Stories of ghosting often deal with broken-off romances, but it's worth mentioning that friend ghosting is real, and sometimes necessary.

definitions.definition: The phenomenon of the writing on one side of a page in a notebook being partly visible on the other side.
An intense, blinding light.
Showy brilliance; gaudiness.
An angry or fierce stare.
A call collision; the situation where an incoming call occurs at the same time as an outgoing call.
A smooth, bright, glassy surface.
definitions.example: a glare of ice

definitions.definition: A viscous, transparent substance; glair.
To stare angrily.
definitions.example: He walked in late, with the teacher glaring at him the whole time.

definitions.definition: To shine brightly.
definitions.example: The sun glared down on the desert sand.

definitions.definition: To be bright and intense, or ostentatiously splendid.
To shoot out, or emit, as a dazzling light.
(of ice) smooth and bright or translucent; glary
definitions.example: skating on glare ice

definitions.definition: The visible horizontal line or point (in all directions) that appears to connect the Earth to the sky.
definitions.example: A tall building was visible on the horizon.

definitions.definition: The range or limit of one's knowledge, experience or interest; a boundary or threshold.
definitions.example: Some students take a gap year after finishing high school to broaden their horizons.

definitions.definition: The range or limit of any dimension in which one exists.
A specific layer of soil or strata
A cultural sub-period or level within a more encompassing time period.
Any level line or surface.
The point at which a computer chess algorithm stops searching for further moves.
The land immediately next to, and inland from, a coast.
The rural territory surrounding an urban area, especially a port.
A remote or undeveloped area, a backwater.
That which is unknown or unexplored about someone.
Anything vague or ill-defined, especially something that is ill understood.
Having the sun at the center/centre; usually in reference to a solar system or orbit.
The part of the forelimb below the forearm or wrist in a human, and the corresponding part in many other animals.
definitions.example: Her hands are really strong.

definitions.definition: That which resembles, or to some extent performs the office of, a human hand.
In linear measurement:
A side; part, camp; direction, either right or left.
Power of performance; means of execution; ability; skill; dexterity.
An agent; a servant, or manual laborer, especially in compounds; a workman, trained or competent for special service or duty; a performer more or less skillful.
definitions.example: an old hand at speaking;  large farms need many farm hands

definitions.definition: An instance of helping.
definitions.example: Bob gave Alice a hand to move the furniture.

definitions.definition: Handwriting; style of penmanship.
definitions.example: a good hand

definitions.definition: A person's autograph or signature.
definitions.example: Given under my Hand and Seal of the State this 1st Day of January, 2010.

definitions.definition: Personal possession; ownership.
(usually in the plural, hands) Management, domain, control.
definitions.example: in safe hands;  in good hands;  He lost his job when the factory changed hands.  With the business back in the founder's hands, there is new hope for the company.  With John in charge of the project, it's in good hands.

definitions.definition: That which is, or may be, held in a hand at once.
Applause.
definitions.example: Give him a hand.

definitions.definition: A Native American gambling game, involving guessing the whereabouts of bits of ivory or similar, which are passed rapidly from hand to hand.
The small part of a gunstock near the lock, which is grasped by the hand in taking aim.
A whole rhizome of ginger.
The feel of a fabric; the impression or quality of the fabric as judged qualitatively by the sense of touch.
definitions.example: This fabric has a smooth, soft hand.

definitions.definition: Actual performance; deed; act; workmanship; agency; hence, manner of performance.
Agency in transmission from one person to another.
definitions.example: to buy at first hand (from the producer, or when new);  to buy at second hand (when no longer in the producer’s hand, or when not new);  It's not a rumor. I heard it at first hand.

definitions.definition: Rate; price.
To give, pass, or transmit with the hand, literally or figuratively.
definitions.example: He handed them the letter.   She handed responsibility over to her deputy.

definitions.definition: To lead, guide, or assist with the hand; to conduct.
definitions.example: to hand a lady into a carriage

definitions.definition: To manage.
To seize; to lay hands on.
To pledge by the hand; to handfast.
(said of a sail) To furl.
To cooperate.
An elevated location smaller than a mountain.
definitions.example: The park is sheltered from the wind by a hill to the east.

definitions.definition: A sloping road.
definitions.example: You need to pick up speed to get up the hill that's coming up.

definitions.definition: A heap of earth surrounding a plant.
A single cluster or group of plants growing close together, and having the earth heaped up about them.
definitions.example: a hill of corn or potatoes

definitions.definition: The pitcher’s mound.
To form into a heap or mound.
To heap or draw earth around plants.
Any reptile of the order Squamata that is not a snake, usually having four legs, external ear openings, movable eyelids and a long slender body and tail.
(chiefly in attributive use) Lizard skin, the skin of these reptiles.
An unctuous person.
A coward.

(rock paper scissors) A hand forming a "D" shape with the tips of the thumb and index finger touching (a handshape resembling a lizard), that beats paper and Spock and loses to rock and scissors in rock-paper-scissors-lizard-Spock.
(in compounds) A person who idly spends time in a specified place, especially a promiscuous female.
definitions.example: lounge lizard; lot lizard; beach lizard; truck stop lizard

definitions.definition: A small stone artifact, probably an arrowhead, with a blunt straight edge and a sharpened, crescent-shaped back, especially characteristic of the Mesolithic Period
The lunate bone
Shaped like a crescent.
definitions.example: a lunate beak

definitions.definition: A sunken panel or coffer in a ceiling or a soffit.
A ceiling containing panels of this kind.
Of or pertaining to a lacuna
A South American mammal of the camel family, Lama glama, used as a domestic beast of burden and a source of wool and meat.
A small stream of running water; a channel for water; a drain.
A large, landlocked stretch of water.
A large amount of liquid; as, a wine lake.
A pit, or ditch
An offering, sacrifice, gift.
Play; sport; game; fun; glee.
To present an offering.
To leap, jump, exert oneself, play.
A kind of fine, white linen.
In dyeing and painting, an often fugitive crimson or vermillion pigment derived from an organic colorant (cochineal or madder, for example) and an inorganic, generally metallic mordant.
In the composition of colors for use in products intended for human consumption, made by extending on a substratum of alumina, a salt prepared from one of the certified water-soluble straight colors.
definitions.example: For example, the name of a lake prepared by extending the aluminum salt prepared from FD&C Blue No. 1 upon the substratum would be FD&C Blue No. 1--Aluminum Lake.

definitions.definition: To make lake-red.
Luxuriant, and ostentatiously magnificent.
Rich, sumptuous and extravagant.
A person, especially a minor, both or (rarely) one of whose parents have died.
A person, especially a minor, whose parents have permanently abandoned them.
A young animal with no mother.
Anything that is unsupported, as by its source, provider or caretaker, by reason of the supporter's demise or decision to abandon.
A single line of type, beginning a paragraph, at the bottom of a column or page.
Any unreferenced object.
To deprive of parents (used almost exclusively in the passive)
definitions.example: What do you do when you come across two orphaned polar bear cubs?

definitions.definition: To make unavailable, as by removing the last remaining pointer or reference to.
definitions.example: Removing categories orphans pages from the main category tree.

definitions.definition: Deprived of parents (also orphaned).
definitions.example: She is an orphan child.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Remaining after the removal of some form of support.
definitions.example: With its government funding curtailed, the gun registry became an orphan program.

definitions.definition: A window or other opening that has an oval or circular shape (as of an eye).
definitions.example: One can glimpse the sky through the oculus of the Pantheon in Rome.

definitions.definition: A tree, Olea europaea, cultivated since ancient times in the Mediterranean for its fruit and the oil obtained from it.
The small oval fruit of this tree, eaten ripe (usually black) or unripe (usually green).
The wood of the olive tree.
A dark yellowish-green color, that of an unripe olive.
An olivary body, part of the medulla oblongata.
A component of a plumbing compression joint; a ring which is placed between the nut and the pipe and compressed during fastening to provide a seal.
A small slice of meat seasoned, rolled up, and cooked.
definitions.example: a beef olive

definitions.definition: Any shell of the genus Oliva and allied genera; so called from the shape.
An oystercatcher, a shore bird.
Of a grayish green color, that of an unripe olive.
A theatrical work, combining drama, music, song and sometimes dance.
The score for such a work.
A building designed for the performance of such works; an opera house.
A company dedicated to performing such works.
(by extension) Any showy, melodramatic or unrealistic production resembling an opera.
A beast of burden, much used in desert areas, of the genus Camelus.
A light brownish color, like that of a camel (also called camel brown).
Loaded vessels lashed tightly, one on each side of another vessel, and then emptied to reduce the draught of the ship in the middle.

A ruminant, of the genus Giraffa, of the African savannah with long legs and highly elongated neck, which make it the tallest living animal; yellow fur patterned with dark spots, often in the form of a network; and two or more short, skin-covered horns, so-called; strictly speaking the horn-like projections are ossicones.
A giraffe unicycle.
A laugh.
definitions.example: Are you having a giraffe?!

definitions.definition: Precept or warning against evil or danger of any kind; exhortation to wariness; advice; injunction; prudence in regard to danger; provident care
A careful attention to the probable effects of an act, in order that failure or harm may be avoided
definitions.example: The guideline expressed caution against excessive radiographic imaging.

definitions.definition: Security; guaranty; bail.
One who draws attention or causes astonishment by their behaviour.
definitions.example: Oh, that boy, he's a caution! He does make me laugh.

definitions.definition: A formal warning given as an alternative to prosecution in minor cases.
A yellow card.
To warn; to alert, advise that caution is warranted.
To give a yellow card
A waterfall or series of small waterfalls.
A stream or sequence of a thing or things occurring as if falling like a cascade.
A series of electrical (or other types of) components, the output of any one being connected to the input of the next; See also daisy chain
A pattern typically performed with an odd number of props, where each prop is caught by the opposite hand.

A sequence of absurd short messages posted to a newsgroup by different authors, each one responding to the most recent message and quoting the entire sequence to that point (with ever-increasing indentation).
A hairpiece for women consisting of curled locks or a bun attached to a firm base, used to create the illusion of fuller hair.
A series of reactions in which the product of one becomes a reactant in the next
To fall as a waterfall or series of small waterfalls.
To arrange in a stepped series like a waterfall.
To occur as a causal sequence.
To vomit.
A shrub belonging to the section Vaccinium sect. Oxycoccus of the genus Vaccinium.
The edible red berry of that shrub.
An intense red colour, like that of a cranberry
A large building that is fortified and contains many defences; in previous ages often inhabited by a nobleman or king.
An instance of castling.
A rook; a chess piece shaped like a castle tower.
A defense structure in shogi formed by defensive pieces surrounding the king.
A close helmet.
Any strong, imposing, and stately mansion.
A small tower, as on a ship, or an elephant's back.
The wicket.
To house or keep in a castle.
To protect or separate in a similar way.
To make into a castle: to build in the form of a castle or add (real or imitation) battlements to an existing building.

(usually intransitive) To move the king 2 squares right or left and, in the same turn, the nearest rook to the far side of the king. The move now has special rules: the king cannot be in, go through, or end in check; the squares between the king and rook must be vacant; and neither piece may have been moved before castling.
(usually intransitive) To create a similar defensive position in Japanese chess through several moves.
To bowl a batsman with a full-length ball or yorker such that the stumps are knocked over.
A hard, calcareous structure present in the mouth of many vertebrate animals, generally used for eating.
A sharp projection on the blade of a saw or similar implement.
A projection on the edge of a gear that meshes with similar projections on adjacent gears, or on the circumference of a cog that engages with a chain.
A projection or point in other parts of the body resembling the tooth of a vertebrate animal.
A pointed projection from the margin of a leaf.
The rough surface of some kinds of cel or other films that allows better adhesion of artwork.
Liking, fondness (compare toothsome).
definitions.example: I have a sweet tooth: I love sugary treats.

definitions.definition: An irreducible component of a comb that intersects the handle in exactly one point, that point being distinct from the unique point of intersection for any other tooth of the comb.
To provide or furnish with teeth.
To indent; to jag.
definitions.example: to tooth a saw

definitions.definition: To lock into each other, like gear wheels.
(applied sciences) the science, and associated technology, of the automatic recording and transmission of data from a remote source to a receiving station for analysis
A conclusive success following an effort, conflict, or confrontation of obstacles; victory; conquest.
definitions.example: the triumph of knowledge

definitions.definition: A magnificent and imposing ceremonial performed in honor of a victor.
Any triumphal procession; a pompous exhibition; a stately show or pageant.
A state of joy or exultation at success.
A trump card.
A card game, also called trump.
A ceremony held to publicly celebrate and sanctify the military achievement of an army commander.
A work of art, cuisine, etc. of very high quality.
definitions.example: Scorsese's latest film is a triumph.

definitions.definition: Not or no longer wild; domesticated
definitions.example: They have a tame wildcat.

definitions.definition: (chiefly of animals) Mild and well-behaved; accustomed to human contact
definitions.example: The lion was quite tame.

definitions.definition: Not exciting.
definitions.example: For a thriller, that film was really tame.

definitions.definition: Crushed; subdued; depressed; spiritless.
(of a knot) Capable of being represented as a finite closed polygonal chain.
One who tames or subdues.
A canopy over a bed.
Something that overhangs something else; especially a canopy or soundboard over a pulpit.
Any muscle having two heads.
Specifically, the biceps brachii, the flexor of the elbow.
The upper arm, especially the collective muscles of the upper arm.
A point in a metrical pattern that can be filled either with one long syllable (a longum) or two short syllables (two brevia)
A medieval treatise of various real or imaginary animals.
A covering for a window to keep out light. The covering may be made of cloth or of narrow slats that can block light or allow it to pass.
A destination sign mounted on a public transport vehicle displaying the route destination, number, name and/or via points, etc.
Any device intended to conceal or hide.
definitions.example: a duck blind

definitions.definition: Something to mislead the eye or the understanding, or to conceal some covert deed or design; a subterfuge.
A blindage.
A halting place.
The blindside.
(1800s) No score.
A forced bet: the small blind or the big blind.
definitions.example: The blinds are $10/$20 and the ante is $1.

definitions.definition: A player who is forced to pay such a bet.
definitions.example: The blinds immediately folded when I reraised.

definitions.definition: (as a plural) Those who are blind, taken as a group.
To make temporarily or permanently blind.
definitions.example: Don't wave that pencil in my face - do you want to blind me?

definitions.definition: To curse.
To darken; to obscure to the eye or understanding; to conceal.
To cover with a thin coating of sand and fine gravel, for example a road newly paved, in order that the joints between the stones may be filled.
(of a person or animal) Unable to see, due to physiological or neurological factors.
(of an eye) Unable to be used to see, due to physiological or neurological factors.
Failing to see, acknowledge, perceive.
definitions.example: Authors are blind to their own defects.

definitions.definition: Of a place, having little or no visibility.
definitions.example: a blind corner

definitions.definition: Closed at one end; having a dead end
definitions.example: a blind gut

definitions.definition: Having no openings for light or passage.
definitions.example: a blind alley

definitions.definition: (in certain phrases) Smallest or slightest.
definitions.example: I shouted, but he didn't take a blind bit of notice.

definitions.definition: Without any prior knowledge.
definitions.example: He took a blind guess at which fork in the road would take him to the airport.

definitions.definition: Unconditional; without regard to evidence, logic, reality, accidental mistakes, extenuating circumstances, etc.
definitions.example: blind deference

definitions.definition: Unintelligible or illegible.
definitions.example: a blind passage in a book; blind writing

definitions.definition: Abortive; failing to produce flowers or fruit.
definitions.example: blind buds

definitions.definition: Without seeing; unseeingly.
Absolutely, totally.
definitions.example: to swear blind

definitions.definition: (three card brag) Without looking at the cards dealt.
A sport in which two opposing teams of five players strive to put a ball through a hoop.
The particular kind of ball used in the sport of basketball.
A flower, especially one indicating that a fruit tree is fruiting; a mass of such flowers.
definitions.example: The blossom has come early this year.

definitions.definition: The state or season of producing such flowers.
definitions.example: The orchard is in blossom.

definitions.definition: A blooming period or stage of development; something lovely that gives rich promise.
The colour of a horse that has white hairs intermixed with sorrel and bay hairs.
To have, or open into, blossoms; to bloom.
To begin to thrive or flourish.
Any plant.
A plant raised for some edible part of it, such as the leaves, roots, fruit or flowers, but excluding any plant considered to be a fruit, grain, herb, or spice in the culinary sense.
The edible part of such a plant.
A person whose brain (or, infrequently, body) has been damaged so that they cannot interact with the surrounding environment; a person in a persistent vegetative state.
Of or relating to plants.
Of or relating to vegetables.
Cloudy diffused matter such as mist, steam or fumes suspended in the air.
The gaseous state of a substance that is normally a solid or liquid.
Something insubstantial, fleeting, or transitory; unreal fancy; vain imagination; idle talk; boasting.
Any medicinal agent designed for administration in the form of inhaled vapour.
(in the plural) Hypochondria; melancholy; the blues; hysteria, or other nervous disorder.
Wind; flatulence.
To become vapor; to be emitted or circulated as vapor.
To turn into vapor.
definitions.example: to vapor away a heated fluid

definitions.definition: To emit vapor or fumes.
To use insubstantial language; to boast or bluster.
To give (someone) the vapors; to depress, to bore.
A piece of paper that entitles the holder to a discount, or that can be exchanged for goods and services.
A receipt.
One who or that which vouches.
A copy of a published advertisement sent by the agency to the client as proof of publication.
A mechanical device used in shops for automatically registering the amount of money drawn.
To establish the authenticity of; to vouch for.
To provide a vouch for (an expenditure).
To provide (a beneficiary) with a voucher.
Any one of various common types of small insects or animals which cause harm and annoyance.
definitions.example: The area was plagued by all sorts of vermin: fleas, lice, mice, and rats to name a few.

definitions.definition: Animals that prey on game, such as foxes or weasels.
Obnoxious, or mean and offensive person or people.
definitions.example: Bring these vermin to the Palace of Justice.

definitions.definition: Against; in opposition to.
definitions.example: It is the Packers versus the Steelers in the Super Bowl.

definitions.definition: Compared with, as opposed to.

Bringing a legal action against, as used in the title of a court case in which the first party indicates the plaintiff (or appellant or the like), and the second indicates the defendant (or respondent or the like).
definitions.example: Brown v. Board of Education of Topeka, Kans.
